Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Tmux Session History + `two` Resurrect Fix

## Context

Three related improvements to the tmux workflow:

1. **`_two_function` resurrect gap** — `_two_function` only runs resurrect restore when the work session does _not_ exist. `_tss_function` (work branch) runs restore _first_, then checks existence. `_two_function` should match that behavior so reconnecting always replays any saved state.

2. **Session history log** — No persistent record of which sessions/windows were active. Adding a 30-second background snapshot to a log file enables "where was I?" lookups via fzf.

3. **`_tsh_function` search** — A fish function to fzf-search the history log and optionally jump to a still-existing session/window.

---

## Changes

### 1. Fix `_two_function` — always resurrect first

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_two_function.fish`

Move the resurrect restore call to _before_ the session-existence check:

```fish
function _two_function --description "Attach to tmux work session"
  set -l restore (tmux list-keys 2>/dev/null | string match -rg '(/\S+/resurrect/scripts/restore\.sh)')
  set -l restore $restore[1]
  if test -n "$restore"
    tmux run-shell "$restore"
  end

  if tmux has-session -t work 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t work
    else
      tmux attach-session -t work
    end
  else
    tmuxinator start work
  end
end
```

---

### 2. Session logger script

**File to add via `home.file`:** `~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh`

Declare in **`home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix`** using `home.file`:

```nix
home.file.".config/tmux/session-logger.sh" = {
  executable = true;
  text = ''
    #!/usr/bin/env sh
    mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux
    LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
    while true; do
      sleep 30
      tmux list-windows -a \
        -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
        >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null
      # Trim to last 10 000 lines to cap file size
      if [ "$(wc -l < "$LOG")" -gt 10000 ]; then
        tail -n 10000 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"
      fi
    done
  '';
};
```

Start it when the tmux server boots — add to **`home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`**:

```
# Persistent session history logger (starts once per server boot)
set-hook -g 'server-startup' 'run-shell -b "~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh"'
```

---

### 3. New `_tsh_function` — search session history

**File to create:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish`

```fish
function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux session history log"
  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
  if not test -f "$log"
    echo "No session history found at $log"
    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  # Field 2 is "session:window_index"
  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end
```

Register in **`home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix`**:
- Add `"_tsh_function"` to the `xdg.configFile` function list (alphabetically between `_tss_function` and `_twf_function`)
- Add `tsh = "_tsh_function";` to `shellAbbrs`

---

## Files to Modify / Create

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_two_function.fish` | Move resurrect to before session-existence check |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` | Add `home.file` for `session-logger.sh` |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` | Add `server-startup` hook to launch logger |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish` | **create** — session history search |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` | Add `_tsh_function` to list + `tsh` abbr |

---

## Verification

1. `two` with no work session → resurrect runs first, work session restored or tmuxinator starts
2. `two` with existing work session → resurrect runs silently, then attaches (no harm if nothing to restore)
3. After tmux server boots, `~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log` starts populating after 30s
4. `tsh` → fzf shows history newest-first → select an entry → switches to still-existing session/window; prints error for dead sessions
5. Log file stays under 10 000 lines after many saves

# From: https://github.com/caarlos0/dotfiles/blob/f9d985c3e8817f8fca133d908a910f173ea933d6/modules/tmux/tmux.conf
# License: MIT

# Color Mode
set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
setenv -g COLORTERM "truecolor"

set -s escape-time 0
setw -g mouse on

# Fix SSH agent after reconnecting
# https://blog.testdouble.com/posts/2016-11-18-reconciling-tmux-and-ssh-agent-forwarding/
set -g update-environment "DISPLAY SSH_ASKPASS SSH_AGENT_PID SSH_CONNECTION WINDOWID XAUTHORITY WAYLAND_DISPLAY SWAYSOCK"
setenv -g SSH_AUTH_SOCK $HOME/.ssh/ssh_auth_sock

# Ensure window index numbers get reordered on delete.
set-option -g renumber-windows on

# Auto rename tmux window to current cwd
set-option -g status-interval 1
set-option -g automatic-rename on
set-option -g automatic-rename-format '#{b:pane_current_path}'

# Vim-mode copy/paste
set-window-option -g mode-keys vi
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'

# Open splits and windows in the current folder
bind u split-window -l 30% -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind i split-window -l 40% -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Repeat time
set-option -g repeat-time 1000

# Clipboard
set -g set-clipboard on

# Status bar
set -g status "on"
set -g status-justify "left"
set -g status-left-length "100"
set -g status-right-length "100"
set -g status-left ""
set -g status-left-style NONE
set -g status-right-style NONE

# Reload tmux config
bind t source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf

# Default shell
set -gu default-command
set -g default-shell "$SHELL"

# Rename session and window
bind r command-prompt -I "#{window_name}" "rename-window '%%'"
bind R command-prompt -I "#{session_name}" "rename-session '%%'"

# Copy text on select
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "reattach-to-user-namespace pbcopy"

# Switch window
bind , previous-window
bind . next-window

# Move pane bindings
bind-key h   select-pane -L
bind-key j   select-pane -D
bind-key k   select-pane -U
bind-key l   select-pane -R

# Swap window bindings
bind-key -r "<" swap-window -d -t -1
bind-key -r ">" swap-window -d -t +1

# Resize pane bindings
bind-key -r H resize-pane -L 5
bind-key -r J resize-pane -D 5
bind-key -r K resize-pane -U 5
bind-key -r L resize-pane -R 5

# Split pane bindings
bind-key "|" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "\\" split-window -fh -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "-" split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "_" split-window -fv -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "%" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Yank pane history to clipboard
bind y run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -pS - | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Full pane history copied to clipboard"'
bind v run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -p | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Visible pane copied to clipboard"'

# Pane number indicator
set -g display-panes-colour colour233
set -g display-panes-active-colour colour245

# Clock mode
set -g clock-mode-colour colour39
set -g clock-mode-style 24

# =============================================================================
# Status bar — clean green theme
# =============================================================================
set -g status-style "bg=colour22,fg=colour255"
set -g status-left "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #S #[bg=colour22,fg=colour28] "
set -g status-right "#[fg=colour250]#{?#{pane_current_command},#{pane_current_command},} #[fg=colour28]| #[fg=colour255]%Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "
set -g status-left-length 30
set -g status-right-length 60

# Window status
set -g window-status-format " #I:#W "
set -g window-status-current-format "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #I:#W "
set -g window-status-separator ""

# Pane border
set -g pane-border-style "fg=colour238"
set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=colour82,bold"

# Message style
set -g message-style "bg=colour28,fg=colour255"

# =============================================================================
# Plugin configuration
# =============================================================================
# Resurrect + Continuum (session persistence)
set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'

# Tmux-thumbs (quick text copy)
set -g @thumbs-key Space

# Sessionx (session manager)

function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux session history log"
  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
  if not test -f "$log"
    echo "No session history found at $log"
    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  # Field 2 is "session:window_index"
  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end

function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux session history log"
  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
  if not test -f "$log"
    echo "No session history found at $log"
    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  # Field 2 is "session:window_index"
  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end

## How it works — two separate systems

### 1. tmux-resurrect + tmux-continuum (session state persistence)
- `@continuum-save-interval '15'` — saves the full tmux state every 15 minutes to `~/.local/share/tmux/resurrect/`
- Saves: all sessions, all windows, all panes, layouts, cwd, and pane contents (because `@resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'`)
- Restores on `two` via the resurrect script
- **This is full restore — every session you have open is saved and restored**

### 2. Session-history logger (the new thing)
Every 30 seconds, `session-logger.sh` runs:
```
tmux list-windows -a -F "timestamp  session:index  window_name  path"
```
The `-a` flag means **all sessions** — every session you have open is logged, not just `work`. So yes, all your sessions are already covered.

A log line looks like:
```
2026-02-26T10:15:30  work:0  dotfiles  /Users/shunkakinoki/dotfiles
2026-02-26T10:15:30  work:1  myapp     /Users/shunkakinoki/projects/myapp
2026-02-26T10:15:30  personal:0  notes  /Users/shunkakinoki/notes
```

### What `tsh` can search

`tsh` fuzzy-searches those log lines with fzf. You can match on:
- **Session name** (`work`, `personal`, …)
- **Window/directory name** (`dotfiles`, `myapp`, …)
- **Full path** (`/Users/shunkakinoki/projects/…`)
- **Timestamp**

### What `tsh` cannot search

**Pane output/terminal text** — the log only captures metadata (name + path), not what was actually printed in the terminal. If you want "find the window where I ran that cargo build", you'd need to also capture pane contents with `tmux capture-pane -p`.

---

**Summary:** all sessions are already logged. `tsh` searches by session, directory name, and path — that covers most "where was I?" cases. If you need to search actual terminal output, we'd need to extend the logger to call `tmux capture-pane` per pane, which gets expensive (the log would grow much faster). Want that?

home.file.".config/tmux/session-logger.sh" = {
    executable = true;
    text = ''
      #!/usr/bin/env sh
      mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
      LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
      PANE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/panes
      while true; do
        sleep 30

        tmux list-windows -a \
          -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
          >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null

        # Trim to last 10 000 lines to cap file size
        if [ "$(wc -l < "$LOG")" -gt 10000 ]; then
          tail -n 10000 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"
        fi

        # Capture full scrollback for every pane across all sessions.
        # Files are named by numeric pane ID (unique per server lifetime) so
        # they are overwritten each cycle — disk use stays bounded.
        tmux list-panes -a -F "#{pane_id} #{session_name} #{window_index}" 2>/dev/null \
          | while IFS= read -r line; do
              pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
              sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
              widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
              num=''${pane_id#%}
              {
                printf '# session=%s window=%s pane=%s\n' "$sess" "$widx" "$pane_id"
                tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null
              } > "$PANE_DIR/$num.txt"
            done
      done
    '';
  };

function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux session history log or pane contents"
  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
  set -l pane_dir ~/.local/share/tmux/panes

  # With argument(s): grep pane content files then jump to the match
  if test (count $argv) -gt 0
    if not test -d "$pane_dir"
      echo "No pane content store found at $pane_dir"
      return
    end

    set -l query (string join ' ' $argv)
    set -l selected (rg -l -- "$query" "$pane_dir" 2>/dev/null \
      | fzf --prompt="pane-search> " \
            --height=40% \
            --preview="rg -n -- '$query' {} 2>/dev/null | head -80" \
            --preview-window=right:60%)

    if test -z "$selected"
      return
    end

    # First line of each pane file: "# session=<name> window=<idx> pane=<id>"
    set -l meta (head -n 1 "$selected")
    set -l sess (string match -rg 'session=(\S+)' $meta)
    set -l widx (string match -rg 'window=(\d+)' $meta)

    if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
      echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
      return
    end

    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
      tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
    end
    return
  end

  # No argument: fzf over the history log (session/window/path metadata)
  if not test -f "$log"
    echo "No session history found at $log"
    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end

    executable = true;
    text = ''
      #!/usr/bin/env sh
      mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/panes

              sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
              widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
              num=''${pane_id#%}

                tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null
              } > "$PANE_DIR/$num.txt"
            done
      done
    '';
  };

function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux session history log or pane contents"
  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log

  # With argument(s): capture live pane content into a tmpdir, search, then discard
  if test (count $argv) -gt 0
    set -l tmpdir (mktemp -d)
    set -l query (string join ' ' $argv)

    tmux list-panes -a -F "#{pane_id} #{session_name} #{window_index}" 2>/dev/null \
      | while read -l line
          set -l fields (string split ' ' $line)
          set -l pane_id $fields[1]
          set -l sess $fields[2]
          set -l widx $fields[3]
          set -l num (string replace '%' '' $pane_id)
          printf '# session=%s window=%s pane=%s\n' $sess $widx $pane_id > "$tmpdir/$num.txt"
          tmux capture-pane -pt $pane_id -S - 2>/dev/null >> "$tmpdir/$num.txt"
        end

    set -l selected (rg -l -- "$query" "$tmpdir" 2>/dev/null \
      | fzf --prompt="pane-search> " \
            --height=40% \
            --preview="rg -n -- '$query' {} 2>/dev/null | head -80" \
            --preview-window=right:60%)

    set -l sess ""
    set -l widx ""
    if test -n "$selected"
      set -l meta (head -n 1 "$selected")
      set sess (string match -rg 'session=(\S+)' $meta)
      set widx (string match -rg 'window=(\d+)' $meta)
    end

    rm -rf "$tmpdir"

    if test -z "$sess"
      return
    end

    if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
      echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
      return
    end

    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
      tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
    end
    return
  end

  # No argument: fzf over the history log (session/window/path metadata)
  if not test -f "$log"
    echo "No session history found at $log"
    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end

function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux session history log or pane contents"
  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log

  # With argument(s): capture live pane content into a tmpdir, search, then discard
  if test (count $argv) -gt 0
    set -l tmpdir (mktemp -d)
    set -l query (string join ' ' $argv)

    tmux list-panes -a -F "#{pane_id} #{session_name} #{window_index}" 2>/dev/null \
      | while read -l line
          set -l fields (string split ' ' $line)
          set -l pane_id $fields[1]
          set -l sess $fields[2]
          set -l widx $fields[3]
          set -l num (string replace '%' '' $pane_id)
          printf '# session=%s window=%s pane=%s\n' $sess $widx $pane_id > "$tmpdir/$num.txt"
          tmux capture-pane -pt $pane_id -S - 2>/dev/null >> "$tmpdir/$num.txt"
        end

    set -l selected (rg -l -- "$query" "$tmpdir" 2>/dev/null \
      | fzf --prompt="pane-search> " \
            --height=40% \
            --preview="rg -n -- '$query' {} 2>/dev/null | head -80" \
            --preview-window=right:60%)

    set -l sess ""
    set -l widx ""
    if test -n "$selected"
      set -l meta (head -n 1 "$selected")
      set sess (string match -rg 'session=(\S+)' $meta)
      set widx (string match -rg 'window=(\d+)' $meta)
    end

    rm -rf "$tmpdir"

    if test -z "$sess"
      return
    end

    if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
      echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
      return
    end

    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
      tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
    end
    return
  end

  # No argument: fzf over the history log (session/window/path metadata)
  if not test -f "$log"
    echo "No session history found at $log"
    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end

content.text:
`<N>` is the numeric part of tmux's internal pane ID. Tmux assigns each pane a globally-unique ID in the form `%0`, `%1`, `%2`, etc. The logger strips the `%` prefix:

```sh
num=''${pane_id#%}
```

So a pane with ID `%3` becomes `3.txt`. The IDs increment monotonically for the lifetime of the tmux server and are never reused within the same server session, so there are no collisions between panes.

Example pane dir after a while:

```
~/.local/share/tmux/panes/
  0.txt   # work:0.0
  1.txt   # work:0.1
  2.txt   # work:1.0
  3.txt   # personal:0.0
```

Each file starts with the metadata header so `tsh` knows which session/window it belongs to:

```
# session=work window=0 pane=%0
... full scrollback content ...
```

function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux session history log or pane contents"
  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
  set -l pane_dir ~/.local/share/tmux/panes

  # With argument(s): search stored pane content files
  if test (count $argv) -gt 0
    if not test -d "$pane_dir"
      echo "No pane content store found at $pane_dir"
      return
    end

    set -l query (string join ' ' $argv)
    set -l selected (rg -l -- "$query" "$pane_dir" 2>/dev/null \
      | fzf --prompt="pane-search> " \
            --height=40% \
            --preview="rg -n -- '$query' {} 2>/dev/null | head -80" \
            --preview-window=right:60%)

    if test -z "$selected"
      return
    end

    # First line of each pane file: "# session=<name> window=<idx> pane=<id>"
    set -l meta (head -n 1 "$selected")
    set -l sess (string match -rg 'session=(\S+)' $meta)
    set -l widx (string match -rg 'window=(\d+)' $meta)

    if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
      echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
      return
    end

    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
      tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
    end
    return
  end

  # No argument: fzf over the history log (session/window/path metadata)
  if not test -f "$log"
    echo "No session history found at $log"
    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end

# Plan: Tmux Session History + `two` Resurrect Fix

## Context

Three related improvements to the tmux workflow:

1. **`_two_function` resurrect gap** — `_two_function` only runs resurrect restore when the work session does _not_ exist. `_tss_function` (work branch) runs restore _first_, then checks existence. `_two_function` should match that behavior so reconnecting always replays any saved state.

2. **Session history log** — No persistent record of which sessions/windows were active. Adding a 30-second background snapshot to a log file enables "where was I?" lookups via fzf.

3. **`_tsh_function` search** — A fish function to fzf-search the history log and optionally jump to a still-existing session/window.

---

## Changes

### 1. Fix `_two_function` — always resurrect first

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_two_function.fish`

Move the resurrect restore call to _before_ the session-existence check:

```fish
function _two_function --description "Attach to tmux work session"
  set -l restore (tmux list-keys 2>/dev/null | string match -rg '(/\S+/resurrect/scripts/restore\.sh)')
  set -l restore $restore[1]
  if test -n "$restore"
    tmux run-shell "$restore"
  end

  if tmux has-session -t work 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t work
    else
      tmux attach-session -t work
    end
  else
    tmuxinator start work
  end
end
```

---

### 2. Session logger script

**File to add via `home.file`:** `~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh`

Declare in **`home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix`** using `home.file`:

```nix
home.file.".config/tmux/session-logger.sh" = {
  executable = true;
  text = ''
    #!/usr/bin/env sh
    mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux
    LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
    while true; do
      sleep 30
      tmux list-windows -a \
        -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
        >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null
      # Trim to last 10 000 lines to cap file size
      if [ "$(wc -l < "$LOG")" -gt 10000 ]; then
        tail -n 10000 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"
      fi
    done
  '';
};
```

Start it when the tmux server boots — add to **`home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`**:

```
# Persistent session history logger (starts once per server boot)
set-hook -g 'server-startup' 'run-shell -b "~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh"'
```

---

### 3. New `_tsh_function` — search session history

**File to create:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish`

```fish
function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux session history log"
  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
  if not test -f "$log"
    echo "No session history found at $log"
    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  # Field 2 is "session:window_index"
  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end
```

Register in **`home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix`**:
- Add `"_tsh_function"` to the `xdg.configFile` function list (alphabetically between `_tss_function` and `_twf_function`)
- Add `tsh = "_tsh_function";` to `shellAbbrs`

---

## Files to Modify / Create

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_two_function.fish` | Move resurrect to before session-existence check |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` | Add `home.file` for `session-logger.sh` |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` | Add `server-startup` hook to launch logger |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish` | **create** — session history search |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` | Add `_tsh_function` to list + `tsh` abbr |

---

## Verification

1. `two` with no work session → resurrect runs first, work session restored or tmuxinator starts
2. `two` with existing work session → resurrect runs silently, then attaches (no harm if nothing to restore)
3. After tmux server boots, `~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log` starts populating after 30s
4. `tsh` → fzf shows history newest-first → select an entry → switches to still-existing session/window; prints error for dead sessions
5. Log file stays under 10 000 lines after many saves

**Current issues with pane file storage:**

1. **Opaque numeric filenames** (`3.txt`) — not human-readable, not stable across server restarts
2. **No cleanup** — files for closed panes persist indefinitely; after server restart, new panes get the same numeric IDs and overwrite old files (OK) but files from panes that don't exist anymore stay
3. **Metadata header** — the first line `# session=work window=0 pane=%3` is needed to parse the file, but this approach couples the naming to the content in an awkward way


4. **History limit** — `tmux capture-pane -S -` only goes back to the beginning of the scrollback buffer, which defaults to 2000 lines

I'm weighing two main approaches: the current snapshot method with `tmux capture-pane` polling every 30 seconds gives me clean text output and bounded disk usage, but I'm limited by the scrollback buffer. The alternative is using `pipe-pane` for real-time event-driven logging that captures output as it happens.

The key difference is what "full pane contents" actually means. Option A gives me the current scrollback buffer (last N lines), while Option B gives me everything ever output. For searching text with `tsh`, Option B is strictly better since it finds content that's scrolled off, but it requires handling raw ANSI codes and managing file rotation.

I think the pragmatic fix is to improve the current approach: raise the scrollback limit to something like 50,000 lines in the tmux config and use clearer filenames like `session--window--pane.txt` to organize the snapshots.

Updating the implementation...

Actually, I'm realizing there's a potential race condition if I delete all `.txt` files before the snapshot loop runs — `tsh` could try to read a file that's been temporarily deleted. A safer approach would be to only delete files after the snapshot completes, removing any `.txt` files whose corresponding panes no longer exist, rather than wiping everything upfront. I'm overthinking the cleanup strategy—the simple approach of deleting all files then recreating them works fine since the window where files don't exist is just milliseconds. # Plan: Pane Content Storage — Best Approach

## Context

The current pane snapshot approach has three problems:

1. **Opaque filenames** — files named `3.txt`, `7.txt` by numeric pane ID, which resets on server restart and is unreadable
2. **No stale cleanup** — files for closed panes accumulate forever; old IDs from previous server sessions leave ghost files
3. **Shallow scrollback** — `tmux capture-pane -S -` is bounded by `history-limit`, which defaults to only 2 000 lines

The fix is straightforward: readable `session--window--pane.txt` naming, atomic stale-file cleanup each cycle, and a large `history-limit` so the scrollback is deep enough to be genuinely useful.

`pipe-pane` (real-time output streaming) would capture content past the scrollback limit but requires ANSI stripping, unbounded log rotation, and hook plumbing — not worth the complexity given 50 000-line scrollback covers days of typical work.

---

## Changes

### 1. Raise scrollback limit

**File:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`

Add after the existing session/window options:

```
set -g history-limit 50000
```

---

### 2. Rewrite pane snapshot in session-logger

**File:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix`

Replace the pane-capture block inside `session-logger.sh`:

```sh
# Rotate existing snapshots out of the way, capture live panes, then
# delete anything that wasn't refreshed (stale / closed panes).
for f in "$PANE_DIR"/*.txt; do
  [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" "${f%.txt}.old"
done

tmux list-panes -a -F "#{session_name} #{window_index} #{pane_index} #{pane_id}" \
  2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
    sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
    widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
    pidx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
    pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f4)
    tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null \
      > "$PANE_DIR/$sess--$widx--$pidx.txt"
  done

rm -f "$PANE_DIR"/*.old 2>/dev/null
```

Key changes vs current:
- **No metadata header** — info is encoded in the filename itself
- **Readable names** — `work--0--0.txt` instead of `3.txt`
- **Stale cleanup** — rotate-to-`.old`, recreate live, delete `.old`; closed-pane files vanish after one cycle

---

### 3. Update `_tsh_function` to parse filename

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish`

Replace the metadata-header parsing with filename parsing:

```fish
# work--0--0.txt → sess=work, widx=0
set -l fname (string replace -r '.*/' '' "$selected" | string replace '.txt' '')
set -l parts (string split -- '--' $fname)
set -l sess $parts[1]
set -l widx $parts[2]
```

Remove the `head -n 1` / `string match -rg 'session=...'` lines.

---

## Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` | Add `set -g history-limit 50000` |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` | Rewrite pane-capture block in `session-logger.sh` |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tsh_function.fish` | Parse session/window from filename, drop header parsing |

---

## Verification

1. After server restart, `~/.local/share/tmux/panes/` shows `work--0--0.txt` style files
2. Close a pane → after the next 30 s cycle, its `.txt` file is gone
3. `tsh cargo build` → rg finds the file, fzf preview shows matching lines, selecting jumps to session/window
4. Deep scrollback: run a long command, scroll far back, confirm `tsh <text>` still finds it

  set -l log ~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
  if not test -f "$log"

    return
  end

  set -l selected (cat "$log" | fzf \
    --prompt="session-history> " \
    --height=40% \
    --tac \
    --no-sort \
    --preview='echo {}')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[2]
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)

    echo "Session '$sess' no longer exists"
    return
  end

    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"

# Plan: Pane Content Storage — Best Approach

## Context

The current pane snapshot approach has three problems:

1. **Opaque filenames** — files named `3.txt`, `7.txt` by numeric pane ID, which resets on server restart and is unreadable
2. **No stale cleanup** — files for closed panes accumulate forever; old IDs from previous server sessions leave ghost files
3. **Shallow scrollback** — `tmux capture-pane -S -` is bounded by `history-limit`, which defaults to only 2 000 lines

The fix is straightforward: readable `session--window--pane.txt` naming, atomic stale-file cleanup each cycle, and a large `history-limit` so the scrollback is deep enough to be genuinely useful.

`pipe-pane` (real-time output streaming) would capture content past the scrollback limit but requires ANSI stripping, unbounded log rotation, and hook plumbing — not worth the complexity given 50 000-line scrollback covers days of typical work.

---

## Changes

### 1. Raise scrollback limit

**File:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`

Add after the existing session/window options:

```
set -g history-limit 50000
```

---

### 2. Rewrite pane snapshot in session-logger

**File:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix`

Replace the pane-capture block inside `session-logger.sh`:

```sh
# Rotate existing snapshots out of the way, capture live panes, then
# delete anything that wasn't refreshed (stale / closed panes).
for f in "$PANE_DIR"/*.txt; do
  [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" "${f%.txt}.old"
done

tmux list-panes -a -F "#{session_name} #{window_index} #{pane_index} #{pane_id}" \
  2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
    sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
    widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
    pidx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
    pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f4)
    tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null \
      > "$PANE_DIR/$sess--$widx--$pidx.txt"
  done

rm -f "$PANE_DIR"/*.old 2>/dev/null
```

Key changes vs current:
- **No metadata header** — info is encoded in the filename itself
- **Readable names** — `work--0--0.txt` instead of `3.txt`

`pipe-pane` (real-time output streaming) would capture content past the scrollback limit but requires ANSI stripping, unbounded log rotation, and hook plumbing — not worth the complexity given 50 000-line scrollback covers days of typical work.

### 1. Raise scrollback limit

### 2. Standalone script + Nix reference

**Create:** `home-manager/programs/tmux/session-logger.sh`

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env sh
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
PANE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/panes

while true; do
  sleep 30

  # Append window metadata
  tmux list-windows -a \
    -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
    >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null

  # Trim to last 10 000 lines
  if [ "$(wc -l < "$LOG")" -gt 10000 ]; then
    tail -n 10000 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"
  fi

  # Rotate existing snapshots, capture all live panes, delete stale
  for f in "$PANE_DIR"/*.txt; do
    [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" "${f%.txt}.old"
  done

  tmux list-panes -a -F "#{session_name} #{window_index} #{pane_index} #{pane_id}" \
    2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
      sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
      widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
      pidx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
      pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f4)
      tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null \
        > "$PANE_DIR/$sess--$widx--$pidx.txt"
    done

  rm -f "$PANE_DIR"/*.old 2>/dev/null
done
```
